steps.title: Parse the dimensions and relationships
steps.content:
A right rectangular prism's volume depends on three dimensions. From the problem:
  • Height = 9 inches
  • Length of base = $x$ inches
  • Width of base = unknown
The key relationship: length is 7 inches more than width. This translates to $x = \text{width} + 7$. Solving for width: $\text{width} = x - 7$.

steps.title: Apply the volume formula
steps.content:
Substitute all three dimensions into the volume formula:
$$V = x \times (x - 7) \times 9 = 9x(x - 7)$$
This matches choice D — D is correct.
